_readme: Content OS · M2 material source catalog. ONE file, many source objects. Each object's 'type' is api | rss | ingest | brightdata. api/rss/brightdata run IN the function; ingest sources are handed in by n8n. Add a source = add one object, redeploy. NEVER put API keys here — use 'auth_ref' to name a Supabase secret env var (brightdata uses the BRIGHTDATA_API_KEY secret automatically). This file knows NOTHING about pages — page wiring lives in the Supabase tables page_material_sources (api), page_reference_sources (reference harvest / brightdata social), page_article_sources (rss/scrape). Placeholders {region} etc. in 'url' are filled per-pull from each page's row.
_brightdata_readme: type='brightdata' sources call Bright Data's sync scraper endpoint. dataset_id (gd_...) comes from the Bright Data dashboard /cp/datasets — paste it in (placeholders 'gd_REPLACE_*' error defensively until set). bd_input='prompt' = AI-search scraper (keyword becomes the prompt; bd_url = the chat surface the scraper drives, a REQUIRED input alongside prompt). bd_input='url' = scrape the ref pages in page_reference_sources matching 'platform' for the selected pages, then keyword-filter. Bright Data is OPT-IN: it only runs when its source name is listed in the request body's 'sources' (keeps default TMDB search fast/free). field_map keys are best-effort — confirm against the first real scrape (the full raw payload is retained regardless).
_brightdata_collect_vs_discover: CRITICAL (learned 2026-07-16): one gd_ dataset = one scraper GROUP (e.g. 'Instagram - Posts'), and each group exposes 'collect by URL' (you hand it exact item URLs — a tweet, a video, a pin) AND 'discover by ...' (you hand it a seed like a profile/channel and it FINDS the items). Same dataset_id; the mode is a query param on the call: &type=discover_new&discover_by=<method>. Our model is profile-URL -> that page's recent posts, which is DISCOVER for every platform except Facebook — 'Facebook - Pages Posts by Profile URL' is natively profile->posts in collect mode, which is why it worked first try and set a misleading precedent. So a bd_* social entry needs the dataset_id of the POSTS group (not the Profiles group) plus bd_discover_by. The bd_discover_by values below are read off each group's own endpoint list — tiktok/linkedin/instagram are confirmed against the dashboard; the rest still want a check against Bright Data's API docs on first real run (RAZ-36). The in-function sync path never uses these (social is async-only); the n8n harvest workflow does.
_external_id_help: external_id feeds the dedup grain (source, external_id, window_day) — dedup.ts builds its key as source|external_id, so a field_map path that does NOT exist in the real payload silently yields ONE shared key per source per day, and everything after the first item is dropped as a duplicate on the sink='events' path. Verified 2026-07-16: the AI scrapers return no 'response_id' (real keys: prompt, answer_text, url, citations, sources, timestamp, index), so all five map external_id -> prompt = one answer per keyword per day, which is the grain we want. Always confirm external_id resolves against a real payload before trusting a source on the events path.
_field_map_help: field_map keys are OUR SourceEnriched fields; values are the path inside the source's response for one item. Target keys: title (required), summary, entities, image_url, media, url, lang, region, country, topic_tags, published_at, engagement, external_id, kind. Wrap literals as {"const": "..."}. Paths use dot notation. 'image_base' prefixes relative image paths (e.g. TMDB poster_path). 'kind' can also come per-subscription via a page_material_sources param.

sources.name: tmdb_movie_list
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A. {list} = now_playing | upcoming | top_rated (same movie shape). Page rows set params {list, kind(=list), language, region}. Workflow A pulls zh-TW/TW + en-US/US = two page_material_sources rows per list. Auth = TMDB v4 read token (Bearer), Supabase secret TMDB_API_KEY.

sources.name: tmdb_trending
sources.notes: RAZ-23 shadow of Workflow A trending/movie/week. region optional (Workflow A's en-US pull omits it — leave region blank in that page row).

sources.name: tmdb_search
sources.notes: Keyword search source (RAZ-26 autonomous trend enrichment + RAZ-37 manual tool). {query} = trend topic or manual keyword, URL-encoded by fillUrl. search:true marks it usable by the search mode.

sources.name: tmdb_watch_providers
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled). RAZ-24 flexible listings adapter — kinds: provider | now_showing | upcoming | ... watch/providers is PER-MOVIE (needs {movie_id}) and returns results keyed by country (not an array) — wire it as trend/movie-driven enrichment (needs the RAZ-26 routing), not a standalone list.

sources.name: article_rss
sources.notes: SCAFFOLD (disabled). RAZ-25 wires page_article_sources (mode=rss) so {feed_url} is filled per row. Scrape-mode articles arrive via the ingest path (source=article_scrape).

sources.name: reference_harvest
sources.notes: INGEST source (RAZ-36). n8n runs Bright Data, then POSTs {mode:'ingest', source:'reference_harvest', pages:[...], items:[...raw posts...]}. n8n pre-shapes each post's 'engagement' object {likes,comments,shares}. tier=inspiration = reference ONLY, never verbatim (contract guardrail).

sources.name: bd_perplexity
sources.notes: AI-search: Bright Data 'Perplexity Search - search by prompt' (dataset gd_m7dhdot1vw9a7gc1n, wired 2026-07-16). keyword -> prompt. Declared inputs: url(req) + prompt(req) + country, index, export_markdown_file. Opt-in via body.sources=['bd_perplexity'].

sources.name: bd_chatgpt
sources.notes: AI-search: Bright Data 'ChatGPT Search - search by prompt' (dataset gd_m7aof0k82r803d5bjm, wired 2026-07-16). Declared inputs: url(req) + prompt(req) + country, index, require_sources, additional_prompt, web_search. bd_params.web_search=true enables live web search (scraper default is false). Opt-in.

sources.name: bd_gemini
sources.notes: AI-search: Bright Data 'Gemini Search - collect by URL' (dataset gd_mbz66arm2mf9cu856y, wired 2026-07-16). Declared inputs: url(req) + prompt(req) + country, index. No web_search control. Opt-in.

sources.name: bd_google_ai
sources.notes: AI-search: Bright Data 'Google AI Mode Search - collect by URL' (dataset gd_mcswdt6z2elth3zqr2, wired 2026-07-16). Declared inputs: url(req, https://google.com/aimode) + prompt(req) + country, hl (interface language, e.g. 'en' — add via bd_params if a non-English answer is ever wanted). Opt-in.

sources.name: bd_copilot
sources.notes: AI-search: Bright Data 'Bing Copilot Search - collect by URL' (dataset gd_m7di5jy6s9geokz8w, wired 2026-07-16). Declared inputs: url(req) + prompt(req) + country, index. Opt-in.

sources.name: bd_facebook
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Facebook - Pages Posts by Profile URL - collect by URL' (dataset gd_lkaxegm826bjpoo9m5). bd_input=url scrapes page_reference_sources rows (platform=facebook) for the selected pages; num_of_posts=10 recent posts each; keyword relevance via AI-assist. field_map VERIFIED against real output 2026-07-14 (title=page_name, summary=content, engagement=composite likes/num_comments/num_shares). Note: header_image is the page cover (not per-post); post images only in post_external_image when present.

sources.name: bd_instagram
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Instagram - Posts' (dataset gd_lk5ns7kz21pck8jpis, wired 2026-07-16 from instagramurl.png). Use discover_by=url so a profile ref_url yields its posts — 'Discover by url' is the only discover mode this group offers (collect mode wants individual /p/ URLs). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_x
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'X (formerly Twitter) - Posts' (dataset gd_lwxkxvnf1cynvib9co, wired 2026-07-16). Group is correct; use discover_by=profile_url so a profile ref_url yields recent posts (its collect-by-URL mode wants individual /status/ URLs). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_tiktok
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'TikTok - Posts' (dataset gd_lu702nij2f790tmv9h, wired 2026-07-16 from tiktokurl.png). discover_by=profile_url CONFIRMED against the group's own endpoint list (collect/keyword/profile_url/url). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_youtube
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Youtube - Videos posts' (dataset gd_lk56epmy2i5g7lzu0k, wired 2026-07-16). Group is correct; use discover_by=url so a channel ref_url yields its videos (collect mode wants individual watch?v= URLs). Other discover modes on this group: explore, hashtag, keyword, podcast url, search filters. Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_linkedin
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'LinkedIn posts' (dataset gd_lyy3tktm25m4avu764, wired 2026-07-16 from linkedinurl.png). discover_by=profile_url CONFIRMED against the group's own endpoint list (collect/company_url/profile_url/url); use discover_by=company_url instead if a ref_url is a company page rather than a person. Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_reddit
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Reddit - Posts' (dataset gd_lvz8ah06191smkebj4, wired 2026-07-16). Group is correct; use discover_by=subreddit_url so a subreddit ref_url yields its posts (collect mode wants individual /comments/ URLs). Other discover modes: author url, keyword. Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_pinterest
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Pinterest - Posts' (dataset gd_lk0sjs4d21kdr7cnlv, wired 2026-07-16). Group is correct; use discover_by=profile_url so a profile ref_url yields its pins (collect mode wants individual /pin/ URLs). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_quora
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data Quora. No screenshot in the 2026-07-16 batch — dataset_id still needed. Errors defensively until pasted.

sources.name: bd_snapchat
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Snapchat posts' (dataset gd_ma0ydx431w6stl16ge, wired 2026-07-16). EXISTS after all — enabled (was disabled as 'least likely to exist'). Use discover_by=profile (collect mode wants individual /spotlight/ URLs). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_vimeo
sources.notes: Social: Bright Data 'Vimeo - Videos posts' (dataset gd_lxk88z3v1ketji4pn, wired 2026-07-16). EXISTS — enabled (was disabled as unverified). Use discover_by=url (collect mode wants individual video URLs). Opt-in. Confirm field_map vs real output.

sources.name: bd_threads
sources.notes: UNVERIFIED (disabled): no screenshot in the 2026-07-16 batch — confirm Bright Data offers a Threads scraper before enabling. (Snapchat and Vimeo, previously flagged the same way, both turned out to exist.)
